Secondary Annex A Control: A.8.29 Security Testing in Development and Acceptance
Business Risk Summary: Can lead to remote code execution and full server compromise; potential for complete data breach and service disruption
Allows session hijacking and account takeover for any user viewing the affected page; can be used for phishing or credential theft
Single point of failure for account compromise; significantly increases impact of credential leaks or phishing
Allows standard users to access administrative functions; risk of data manipulation
Outdated TLS/cipher support enables interception of data in transit under specific network conditions
Increases feasibility of credential brute-forcing; combined with No MFA this significantly raises account compromise risk
Enables brute-force and credential stuffing attacks at scale; lack of throttling also means abuse may go undetected

Exploitability: Low - requires shared/unattended device or session theft
Business Risk Summary: Extended session validity increases window for session hijacking
Priority: particularly on shared or unattended devices

Exploitability: Low - requires network position and user to bypass warnings
Business Risk Summary: Indicates weak certificate management practices; does not directly enable exploitation but undermines trust validation
Absence of headers like CSP and X-Frame-Options increases susceptibility to clickjacking and content injection attacks
